title: Daily Briefing — May 06
headline: Tech leads, but the rally is getting greedy and breadth is lagging — patience is king.
body: Right, so the big picture: Markets are grinding higher with SPY at $723.77 and a greed-heavy Fear & Greed index now at 67.3. We're well above daily and weekly 20 MAs across indices, volatility is contracting (VIX 16.44 below its SMA20 18.29), and breadth is feeling a bit narrow — classic late-stage bull vibes.

Technology (XLK $165.63) remains the only sector firmly in ‘Leading’ territory, with momentum and relative strength streets ahead. Cyclicals and defensives are lagging or weakening; XLF (Financials) is still below its weekly 20 MA ($52.30) and showing risk signals. Rotation is favouring tech, but it's a ‘one-horse race’ — RSP/SPY divergence is reminiscent of those dot-com narrow rallies.

Contrarian check: Greed is ramping (was 22.5 a month ago — now 67.3), so one more push and I’ll be reducing exposure; sentiment extremes are lurking. If you’re not already long, don’t chase — abundance mindset. Watch NVDA ($196.50, bearish bias) closer to its SMA20 ($199.19), and keep an eye on HYG ($79.92) and LQD ($108.65) as both are drifting below critical levels: if they break, everything breaks (rule R001).
callout_body:
SPY: $723.77 (20 MA $704.68) — still strong, but getting stretched
XLK: $165.63 (20 MA $154.33) — tech leadership, but watch breadth divergence
XLF: $51.59 (weekly 20 MA $52.30) — financials lagging, a canary
NVDA: $196.50 (20 MA $199.19) — bearish bias near support
HYG: $79.92 / LQD: $108.65 — bonds drifting, risky if break
Fear & Greed: 67.3 (reduce if >75 per R013)
action_items: Start prepping to trim tech longs if Fear & Greed hits 75 or VIX spikes — don't chase, wait for mean reversion.
Monitor NVDA and financials (XLF): If NVDA closes below its 20 MA ($199.19) or XLF breaks $51.59, step back — defensive mode per Tom’s rules.
closing_quote: If it’s in the press and everyone’s chasing, it’s already priced. Patience, react — don’t predict.
